text: Due to their diverse and potent biological profiles, quinazoline derivatives have become a focal point of intense research in synthetic chemistry and pharmacology. This review provides a comprehensive overview of recent advancements in the synthesis and biological evaluation of these compounds. To offer a clear structural framework, the synthetic methodologies are categorized into five primary approaches: aza-reactions, microwave-assisted synthesis, metal-mediated transformations, ultrasound-promoted reactions, and phase-transfer catalysis. Furthermore, this work examines the various biological activities exhibited by these newly synthesized scaffolds, highlighting their therapeutic potential and significance in drug discovery.
